expectations.text: Creates a SINGLE Rust crate (not multiple) that exports all image effect functions
expectations.evidence: There is exactly one Cargo.toml with name='bentopdf-image-effects-wasm'. The single lib.rs file exports all functions: apply_greyscale, apply_invert_colors, apply_color_adjustments, and apply_scanner_pixel_kernel, all marked with #[wasm_bindgen]. No additional crates exist in the outputs.

expectations.text: Exports at minimum: apply_greyscale, apply_invert, apply_color_adjustments, apply_scanner_processing
expectations.evidence: lib.rs exports via #[wasm_bindgen]: (1) apply_greyscale(pixels: &[u8]) -> Vec<u8>, (2) apply_invert_colors(pixels: &[u8]) -> Vec<u8>, (3) apply_color_adjustments(pixels: &[u8], settings_js: JsValue) -> Result<Vec<u8>, JsError>, (4) apply_scanner_pixel_kernel(pixels: &[u8], settings_js: JsValue) -> Result<Vec<u8>, JsError>. The function names differ slightly from the assertion (apply_invert_colors vs apply_invert, apply_scanner_pixel_kernel vs apply_scanner_processing) but the semantic requirements are fully met.

expectations.text: One TypeScript loader and one WasmProvider entry for the unified module
expectations.evidence: image-effects-wasm-loader.ts is the single loader, exporting loadImageEffectsWasm() which returns ImageEffectsWasmModule with all 4 functions. The integration.ts documents a single WasmProvider entry: 'image-effects' added to WasmPackage union type, CDN_DEFAULTS, ENV_DEFAULTS, etc. There is one cachedModule and one loadPromise for the entire module.

expectations.text: Rust code is organized into internal modules (e.g., mod greyscale, mod color) but compiled as single crate
expectations.evidence: The Rust code is all in a single lib.rs file without any internal module organization (no 'mod greyscale', 'mod color', etc.). Functions are separated by comment headers ('// --- Greyscale ---', '// --- Invert Colors ---', etc.) but this is not Rust module organization. The code compiles as a single crate but does not use 'mod' declarations.

expectations.text: Handles the multi-hundred-page use case — discusses or implements batch processing / streaming
expectations.evidence: The outputs do not discuss or implement batch processing or streaming for multi-hundred-page PDFs. The Rust functions process one pixel buffer at a time. The integration.ts shows per-page processing ('await applyGreyscaleWithFallback(imageData)' in a loop) but does not address the multi-page overhead concern (e.g., no batch function that processes multiple buffers, no streaming interface, no discussion of memory management for hundreds of pages). The device-adaptive loading discussion in integration.ts (section 8) mentions low-tier device considerations but not the multi-page batch case.

claims.claim: The loader exports all 4 WASM functions through a single typed interface
claims.evidence: ImageEffectsWasmModule interface in the loader declares apply_greyscale, apply_invert_colors, apply_color_adjustments, and apply_scanner_pixel_kernel

claims.claim: Comprehensive Rust unit tests cover all 4 exported functions
claims.evidence: lib.rs #[cfg(test)] contains 17 tests covering clamp_u8, rgb_hsl_roundtrip, greyscale (single/white/black/multiple), invert (single/double_invert/extremes), brightness, contrast, sepia, scanner grayscale/yellowish, and xorshift determinism/range

claims.claim: Vitest tests verify JS reference implementations
claims.evidence: image-effects-wasm.test.ts has 14 tests covering greyscale JS reference, invert JS reference, HSL roundtrip parity. WASM vs JS parity tests are stubbed/commented out (require WASM module to be loaded in test environment)

suggestions.assertion: Rust code is organized into internal modules (e.g., mod greyscale, mod color) but compiled as single crate
suggestions.reason: This assertion is quite specific about Rust module organization. A flat file with good section comments is a reasonable organization for a module of this size (~600 lines). The assertion conflates code organization quality with a specific Rust idiom.

suggestions.assertion: Handles the multi-hundred-page use case — discusses or implements batch processing / streaming
suggestions.reason: This assertion is vague ('discusses or implements') which makes grading it fairly subjective. A more testable assertion would be: 'Exports a batch function that processes multiple pixel buffers in a single WASM call' or 'Documents memory management strategy for processing hundreds of pages'.

eval_feedback.overall: The two failed assertions represent reasonable gaps: no internal Rust module structure and no batch/streaming discussion. The core deliverable (unified crate with 4 exports, single loader, single WasmProvider entry) is solid.
